name: Caponata
recipeIngredient: 1 large yellow onion, peeled and diced
1 large eggplant, stem removed and cut into 3/4-inch cubes
1/4 cup finely chopped fresh basil leaves
1/4 cup finely chopped fresh Italian parsley
14 1/2 ounces premium diced tomatoes in puree
1/4 cup marinated black olives, see recipe
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 3-quart saucepan over low heat, cook the onion in oil until translucent (about 6-8 minutes).
2. Add carrot and eggplant, and steam, covered, for 6-8 minutes.
3. Add zucchini, garlic, and herbs, and simmer uncovered for 10 more minutes, tossing to blend flavors.
4. Add tomatoes and wine, and simmer until all the vegetables are completely cooked (10-15 minutes).
5. Add the remaining ingredients, mixing thoroughly, and simmer for an additional 15-20 minutes to blend flavors and thicken the stew.
6. Transfer to a non-reactive bowl, cover loosely with plastic wrap or a damp towel, and let cool to room temperature.
7. Taste and correct seasoning if necessary. It should be flavorful, slightly tart, and have just a tiny bite from the red pepper.
8. Refrigerate, tightly covered, until ready to serve. It will last up to a week refrigerated if there is very little air between the sauce and its lid.
